title: brassica angustifolia
intro: Brassica angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved cabbage or wild cabbage, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Brassica angustifolia include its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote the removal of mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of Brassica angustifolia are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
